ENGLISH: "I'm not that interesting. I just did like, what most Exiles did. As the VK got all stale, and no one could find a job, we moved downriver. Urth was really cool back in the day, with all that stuff. My dad took me and my sister all the way down. He worked on the GSP, you know? I never got into construction myself. But since I finished school, I've been on the docks. Not here, I've only worked on Waterlane for a year or two. But I've been all over the city, really. The GSP Outlet. Icebay Point. North Housing. Had a gig with a factory on North Wall before the Bone Purge. But they shut down once they took down the zombies. So yeah. Not a huge fan of the Freestriders, but the AR pays well. Nice folks too. Beyond that, I'm looking for a wife at the moment. Had my eyes on this gal who lived in the Hills last year, but she liked my friend Jagge more. I don't blame her, he's got a real nice mustache. Though it's getting hard saving up for a house these days, so not sure if I'll ever afford the whole family life, you know."

"To be apolitical in times of moral crisis is a stance as potent and as political as any other! It's like standing in the middle of a burning library and saying, 'I am not interested in books.' Your apathy, intentional or not, serves the status quo, the very powers that be! To be apolitical is to let the tides of history wash over you, while pretending you're not getting wet. In a world torn between tyrants, traders, and toilers, to choose no side is to side with the strongest current. And beware, my friend, for the strongest current is not always the noblest." He leans back, his voice a mixture of frustration and fervor. "Inaction is an action, silence a statement. By choosing to be apolitical, you cast a vote for the world as it is, not as it could be."
